title: FIRST SOLAR Earnings Results: FSLR Reports Quarterly Earnings
text: First Solar reported record first quarter 2026 results, with earnings per share of $3.22 exceeding the consensus estimate and revenue reaching $1.04 billion. The company's strong performance was driven by robust U.S. module sales at $0.34/watt, record India sales at 1.0 GW, and successful CURE technology launch at the Perrysburg facility. The quarter demonstrated effective execution across multiple areas: U.S. facilities operated at 96% utilization, gross margin expanded by 6 percentage points to 47%, and adjusted EBITDA reached $520 million above guidance expectations. Management reaffirmed 2026 guidance including $4.9-5.2 billion in net sales and $2.6-2.8 billion in adjusted EBITDA. The company highlighted its substantial competitive advantages including proprietary CdTe thin-film technology, 47.9 GW contracted backlog through 2030, domestic manufacturing footprint independent from Chinese supply chains, and Section 45X tax credit monetization capability. Key risks include tariff policy uncertainty, solar demand cyclicality, and execution risks on CURE technology rollout and perovskite development. The company expects the South Carolina finishing facility to begin operations in H2 2026, adding 3.7 GW annual capacity and supporting long-term growth trajectory.
